title: At the Dentist
summary: A patient visits the dentist with a toothache and the dentist explains what needs to be done.

body.speaker: Dentist
body.text: Good morning. Please come in and sit down.

body.speaker: Dentist
body.text: So, what is the problem today?

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: I have a terrible toothache. It started two days ago.

body.speaker: Dentist
body.text: I see. Which tooth hurts? Can you point to it?

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: It is this one, on the left side at the back.

body.speaker: Dentist
body.text: Okay. Open your mouth wide, please. I will take a look.

body.speaker: Dentist
body.text: You have a small hole in the tooth. We call it a cavity.

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: Oh no. Does that mean I need a filling?

body.speaker: Dentist
body.text: Yes, I need to clean the cavity and put in a filling. It will not hurt very much.

body.speaker: Dentist
body.text: Yes, a small one to make the area numb. You will feel a little sting for one second.

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: Okay. How long will it take?

body.speaker: Dentist
body.text: About twenty minutes. After that, you should avoid hard or cold food for a few hours.

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: I understand. Thank you for explaining everything.

body.speaker: Dentist
body.text: Of course. And try to brush your teeth twice a day to prevent future cavities.

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: I will. I promise to take better care of my teeth.

annotations.exampleEn: I have a terrible toothache and cannot sleep.
He had a terrible headache all day.
The dentist found a cavity in my back tooth.
I need a filling because I have a cavity.
The doctor gave me an injection before the operation.
My mouth felt numb for an hour after the injection.
You will feel a small sting when I put in the needle.
You should avoid eating sweets if you want healthy teeth.
You should brush your teeth after every meal.
The doctor said to take this medicine twice a day.
Eating less sugar can help prevent tooth decay.
It is important to take care of your health.

grammarNotes.title: should for advice and recommendations
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Should + verb' dùng để đưa ra lời khuyên hoặc đề nghị: 'You should avoid hard or cold food.' / 'You should brush your teeth twice a day.' Đây là cách nhẹ nhàng hơn 'must' và rất thường gặp trong hội thoại về sức khỏe.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Should + verb' is used to give advice and recommendations: 'You should avoid hard or cold food.' / 'You should brush your teeth twice a day.' It is softer than 'must' and is very common in health-related conversations.

grammarNotes.title: Simple Past for recent events
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ đơn dùng để nói về sự kiện đã xảy ra trước đây: 'It started two days ago.' 'Ago' đi cùng với thì quá khứ đơn để cho biết khoảng cách thời gian từ hiện tại đến khi sự việc xảy ra.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The simple past tense is used to talk about events that happened before now: 'It started two days ago.' 'Ago' is always used with the simple past to indicate the time distance from the present to when the event happened.

translationVi: Nha sĩ: Chào buổi sáng. Mời vào và ngồi xuống. Bệnh nhân: Chào buổi sáng. Cảm ơn bác sĩ. Nha sĩ: Vậy, hôm nay có vấn đề gì? Bệnh nhân: Tôi bị đau răng rất kinh khủng. Nó bắt đầu hai ngày trước. Nha sĩ: Tôi hiểu. Răng nào đau? Bạn có thể chỉ cho tôi không? Bệnh nhân: Cái này, bên trái phía sau. Nha sĩ: Được rồi. Mở miệng rộng ra, làm ơn. Tôi sẽ xem qua. Bệnh nhân: Có nghiêm trọng không ạ? Nha sĩ: Bạn có một lỗ nhỏ ở răng. Chúng tôi gọi đó là sâu răng. Bệnh nhân: Ôi không. Điều đó có nghĩa là tôi cần trám răng không? Nha sĩ: Vâng, tôi cần làm sạch chỗ sâu và trám răng. Sẽ không đau lắm đâu. Bệnh nhân: Bác sĩ có tiêm thuốc không? Nha sĩ: Có, một mũi nhỏ để làm tê vùng đó. Bạn sẽ cảm thấy hơi châm trong một giây. Bệnh nhân: Được rồi. Sẽ mất bao lâu? Nha sĩ: Khoảng hai mươi phút. Sau đó, bạn nên tránh thức ăn cứng hoặc lạnh trong vài giờ. Bệnh nhân: Tôi hiểu rồi. Cảm ơn bác sĩ đã giải thích mọi thứ. Nha sĩ: Không có gì. Và hãy cố đánh răng hai lần mỗi ngày để ngăn ngừa sâu răng trong tương lai. Bệnh nhân: Vâng. Tôi hứa sẽ chăm sóc răng của mình tốt hơn.

questions.prompt: Why does the patient visit the dentist?
questions.hint: Find the patient's answer to the dentist's question.
Find the sentence about when the pain started.

options: On the right side at the front
In the middle at the top
On the left side at the back
At the front at the bottom
questions.explanation: 'It is this one, on the left side at the back.'
questions.hint: Find the patient's description of the tooth location.

questions.prompt: What does the dentist find in the tooth?
options: A small hole called a cavity
questions.explanation: 'You have a small hole in the tooth. We call it a cavity.'

questions.prompt: What treatment does the patient need?
questions.explanation: 'I need to clean the cavity and put in a filling.'

questions.prompt: What does the dentist say about the pain of the treatment?
options: It will not hurt at all
It will not hurt very much
questions.hint: Find what the dentist says to reassure the patient.

questions.prompt: Why does the dentist give an injection?
questions.explanation: 'A small one to make the area numb.'
questions.hint: Find the dentist's explanation of the injection.

questions.prompt: How will the patient feel during the injection?
options: A little sting for one second
questions.explanation: 'You will feel a little sting for one second.'
questions.hint: Find the dentist's warning about the injection.

questions.prompt: How long will the filling take?
questions.hint: Find the dentist's answer about time.

questions.prompt: What should the patient avoid after the filling?
questions.explanation: 'You should avoid hard or cold food for a few hours.'
questions.hint: Find the dentist's advice about food.

questions.prompt: How often does the dentist say the patient should brush her teeth?
questions.explanation: 'Try to brush your teeth twice a day.'
questions.hint: Find the dentist's advice about brushing.

questions.prompt: What does the patient promise at the end?
options: To take better care of her teeth
questions.explanation: 'I promise to take better care of my teeth.'
questions.hint: Look at the patient's last line.

'You have a small hole in the tooth.'
'Does that mean I need a filling?'
'A small one to make the area numb.'
'You should avoid hard or cold food for a few hours.'
'Try to brush your teeth twice a day to prevent future cavities.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The dentist tells the patient to brush her teeth twice a day to prevent cavities. Why do you think many people do not take good care of their teeth even though they know it is important? What can people do to build better health habits?
